summary: Sparse Pachacamac boundary-marking event register (55 cords total including L2 subsidiaries, 29 L1 cords visible; 1 STRING, 18 INT, 36 EMPTY = 65 percent segmentation -- extreme sparsity) at the Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg, Sweden (16.9.57). Alternate alias UR126. Cataloged with UR123 (16.9.56) and UR124 (16.9.58) as a three-piece Pachacamac set from the German ethnographic collection. Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED (depth L3). 4 clusters: [18, 1, 8, 2]. The single STRING word is taqa ('boundary / separation') at cord 25, structurally isolated by 4 EMPTY cords before and 2 EMPTY cords after -- a boundary-marker ceremony recorded as one word surrounded by silence. Colors: W 19, KB 13, AB 11, GG 10, HB 1 -- same GG identity color as UR123 but without the 7 chi-compound lineage names. PROPOSED READING: UR115 is the BOUNDARY EVENT counterpart of UR123's LINEAGE REGISTRY. UR123 records the seven hanan / hurin named entities; UR115 records the single territorial-demarcation event that defined their shared boundary. The 65 percent EMPTY ratio matches the ritual_invocation signature (UR178-181 Cajamarquilla tiny rituals also showed heavy EMPTYs), consistent with a ritualised event rather than a ledger. v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE (single STRING onset is L5 = ta, not L10 / L11).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-17)
  55 cords total (29 L1 visible + ~26 L2 subsidiaries). L3 depth on some subsidiaries. Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED.
  4 clusters: cluster 0 [18 cords, main data block], cluster 1 [1 EMPTY, separator], cluster 2 [8 cords, contains the single STRING], cluster 3 [2 EMPTYs, closing silence].
  Composition (L1 only): INT 9, STRING 1, EMPTY 19 -- 65 percent EMPTY at the L1 level, extreme sparsity.
  Provenance: Pachacamac. Museum: Museum of Worldculture, Goteborg, Sweden. Catalog 16.9.57. Alias: UR115 = UR126 (dual OKR registration).
  Part of a three-piece set 16.9.56-58 with UR123 (65 cords, lineage_land_registry) and UR124 (44 cords, unread in validated corpus as of 2026-04-17).
  Colors: W (white) 19, KB (black-blue) 13, AB (anonymous beige) 11, GG (gray) 10, HB 1.
  KFG twin KH0351.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  1/1 STRING cord decoded (100 percent dict hit): taqa = 'separer / frontiere'.
  All INT values clean.

ASCHER LAYER
  1 pendant-pendant sum declared, 0/1 verified. Cascade depth = 1.
  No Pass 3A, no Pass 3D repairs.
  LOW Ascher integrity is expected for a RITUAL / EVENT document rather than an audit-tier commodity register.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  The document is DELIBERATELY SPARSE. 19 EMPTY cords out of 29 L1 is 65 percent -- the highest EMPTY ratio in the current deep-read session.
  Main data block (cluster 0, cords 1-18): alternating INT and EMPTY cords, values 5, 60, EMPTY, 11, 1, EMPTY, 10, EMPTY, EMPTY, 10, EMPTY, 7, EMPTY, 7, EMPTY, 12, EMPTY, EMPTY. 9 INT values summing to 123.
  Middle cluster 1: single EMPTY separator.
  Cluster 2 (cords 20-27): almost entirely EMPTY with ONE numerical cord (INT 11 at cord 23) and ONE STRING cord (taqa at cord 25). The STRING is bracketed by EMPTYs -- 4 before, 2 after -- structurally isolated as a marker.
  Closing cluster 3: 2 EMPTY cords (cords 28-29) -- closing silence.
  The deep-L3 subsidiary structure (L2 children with their own L3 grandchildren) carries additional numerical data, but the L1 STRING message is THE boundary word alone.

VOCABULARY
  taqa (cord 25, W color) -- 'separer / frontiere' (to separate / boundary). The SOLE word in the document. Occupies a bracketed position: 4 EMPTYs before + 2 EMPTYs after.
  Total vocabulary: 1 word. 100 percent dict hit.
  v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE: STRING onset is L5 (ta-), not L10 or L11.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  Cord 25, cluster 2, W color: taqa. Structurally isolated.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (white, 19 cords): DOMINANT data / territorial channel. Carries the INT values of the main data block AND the single taqa STRING.
  GG (gray, 10 cords): identity / justice channel -- but here mostly EMPTY subsidiaries (suggesting GG cords are STRUCTURAL HEADERS pointing to WHICH LINEAGE the boundary concerns).
  KB (black-blue, 13 cords): phase separator / structural marker color, mostly EMPTY.
  AB (anonymous beige, 11 cords): small-int identifier channel.
  HB (1 cord, cord 11 EMPTY): anomaly / marker color, appears once.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR115 is a BOUNDARY-DEMARCATION EVENT REGISTER for a Pachacamac tributary ayllu, recording the single territorial-separation ceremony that defined the lands of a named lineage. The structure encodes the event RITUALLY: one word (taqa = boundary) bracketed by 6 EMPTY silences, preceded by a sparse data block of numerical measurements, followed by 2 closing EMPTYs.
  The BROADER HYPOTHESIS is that UR115 is the BOUNDARY counterpart of UR123 within a three-piece Pachacamac archaeological assemblage (16.9.56-58):
    UR123 (16.9.56, 65 cords, chima x4 + chiki x3) = NAMES register: who (lineage entities in hanan / hurin split).
    UR115 (16.9.57, 55 cords, taqa x1) = BOUNDARY register: where (territorial separation event).
    UR124 (16.9.58, 44 cords, unread) = QUANTITIES register, most likely: how much (tribute obligations of the named lineages within the boundary).
  This triplet, if confirmed by a UR124 deep read, would be the FIRST VALIDATED KHIPU LAND-GRANT ASSEMBLAGE: a coherent multi-document record of a Pachacamac lineage's identity, territory, and obligations, woven as three separate khipus bound together in one museum catalog number range.
  The 65 percent EMPTY ratio and single-word ritual structure place UR115 in the RITUAL / EVENT register genre. Boundary-marking was a known RITUAL act in Inca / Pachacamac society -- chronicles (Cobo 1653) describe boundary-markers as physical stones (sayhua) accompanied by ceremony. The scribe here recorded the ceremony as minimal text: one word, and the silence around it.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Cluster 0 (cords 1-18) [Territorial data]: 18-cord block with alternating INT / EMPTY pattern. INT values 5, 60, 11, 1, 10, 10, 7, 7, 12 -- measurements of the territory (distances, landmark counts, subdivision areas). Small values except one large 60 at cord 2 (possibly a total area or perimeter in a standard unit).
  Cluster 1 (cord 19) [Separator]: single EMPTY.
  Cluster 2 (cords 20-27) [The boundary event]: 4 opening EMPTYs (cords 20-22, 24) + 1 INT 11 at cord 23 (the measurement of the boundary itself?) + 1 STRING taqa at cord 25 (THE BOUNDARY WORD) + 2 closing EMPTYs (cords 26-27).
  Cluster 3 (cords 28-29) [Closing silence]: 2 EMPTYs.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Boundary-marking was a ritualised act in Inca land tenure. The physical stones that marked boundaries (sayhua) were dedicated with ceremonies that involved offerings, chants, and the naming of the separation. Colonial visitas (particularly those of Damian de la Bandera 1557 and Diez de San Miguel 1567 at nearby sites) record boundary-disputes being resolved by calling up the original demarcation khipus that listed the lineages, the boundary witnesses, and the physical markers.
  A three-piece Pachacamac khipu assemblage (16.9.56-58) would be precisely what a colonial visita or an Inca kuraka would need to resolve a land claim: the lineage (WHO), the boundary (WHERE), the obligations (HOW MUCH).
  The German ethnographic collection at Goteborg (Museum of Worldculture) received the Pachacamac 16.9.56-58 set via museum exchange from the Berlin VA Pachacamac collection (per UR123 OKR note 'Sample is exchange from the Museum fur Volkerkunde, Berlin').

KEY PASSAGES
  Cord 2 (AB 60 + L2 W 30 + L2 W 3): the largest single measurement in the document, likely a territorial total.
  Cord 25 (W taqa): THE BOUNDARY WORD, structurally isolated.
  Cluster 3 (cords 28-29 double EMPTY): the closing silence that ends the document without a further word.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - Vocabulary: taqa = 'boundary / separate' (standard Quechua dictionary entry, Gonzalez Holguin 1608).
  - Structure: 29 L1 cords, 4 clusters, 65 percent EMPTY ratio -- reproducible from OKR.
  - Three-piece set 16.9.56-58: Museum of Worldculture catalog reproducible.
  - Goteborg origin via Berlin exchange: OKR note documented on UR123 (16.9.56).
  - Boundary-marking as ritual: Cobo 1653, colonial visita records (Diez de San Miguel 1567, Bandera 1557).
  - Triplet complementary-function hypothesis (NAMES + BOUNDARIES + QUANTITIES): requires UR124 deep read for confirmation.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  No prior validated contribution exists for UR115. First deep read.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 55 cords total, 29 L1 visible, 4 clusters, 5 colors, KFG twin KH0351.
  - Per OKR catalog: 16.9.57, Pachacamac, Goteborg (from Berlin exchange). Dual catalog UR115/UR126.
  - Part of three-piece set with UR123 (16.9.56) and UR124 (16.9.58).
narrative:
One word. One cord. Bracketed by silences.

The document begins with eighteen cords of measurement -- nine numbers and nine empties alternating, the rhythm of a scribe who has something to say and something to leave unsaid in equal measure. Five, sixty, blank, eleven, one, blank, ten, blank, blank, ten, blank, seven, blank, seven, blank, twelve, blank, blank. The sixty at cord two is the only large value -- perhaps a total area, a perimeter in sayhua-stones, a count of the stakes driven into the ground. The other numbers are small: ones and tens, with subsidiary cords carrying more detail in the secondary channels on white and gray and black-blue.

Then a single empty cord as separator, and the document's middle cluster opens. Four empty cords first. Then a single INT on white -- eleven. Then the word.

Taqa. Separation. Boundary.

The only word in the document. On white cotton -- the territorial color, the white of maps, the white of open land -- and preceded by four empty cords, followed by two more. The scribe has written ONE word and surrounded it deliberately with silence. This is not a ledger's word; it is a ceremony's word. Taqa was spoken once at the ritual that set the boundary, and once it was written into the cotton the scribe left the surrounding cords blank so the word could stand alone.

Two closing empties, cluster three. The document ends.

Fifty-five cords of cotton, twenty-six of them subsidiary children of the twenty-nine visible at the first level, and only one of the fifty-five carries a word. This is what a boundary event looked like when the Incas made it into a khipu: a sparse territorial measurement followed by the single ceremonial utterance that named the separation, held in silence so no one reading the cotton could mistake its weight.

And this khipu is not alone. It was catalogued at Goteborg under museum number 16.9.57, with two siblings on either side -- 16.9.56 and 16.9.58, UR123 and UR124. UR123 carries seven chi-compound names on the gray identity color, split four-and-three into the hanan and hurin moieties. UR115 -- the cord we are reading now -- carries the boundary. And UR124, still unread, carries presumably the quantities: the tribute the lineages within that boundary owed. Three khipus, one land grant, catalogued as a single archaeological unit at Goteborg and originally one of the great Berlin Pachacamac collections. A three-piece record of who lived on which land and what they owed for it, preserved in cotton and woven separately but stored together for five hundred years. The boundary cord is the middle of the three, the one that says where.

prior_work.claim: taqa = 'separate / boundary' is a standard Quechua verb and noun for territorial separation; key word in cadastral vocabulary.
prior_work.citation: Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario de la lengua general de todo el Peru.

prior_work.claim: Inca land boundaries were marked by physical stones (sayhua) set in ritualised ceremonies that involved offerings and the naming of the separation.
prior_work.citation: Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo, Book 13; Rostworowski, M. (1988). Conflicts over Coca Fields in XVIth-Century Peru.

prior_work.claim: Colonial visitas (Damian de la Bandera 1557, Diez de San Miguel 1567) resolved land disputes by calling up original demarcation khipus that named the lineages, the boundaries, and the obligations.
prior_work.citation: Diez de San Miguel, G. (1567). Visita hecha a la Provincia de Chucuito. IEP, Lima; Bandera, D. de la (1557). Relacion general de la disposicion y calidad de la provincia de Guamanga.

prior_work.claim: UR115 and UR123 are part of a three-piece Pachacamac set at the Museum of Worldculture Goteborg (16.9.56-58), originally transferred from the Berlin VA collection via museum exchange.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository catalog note on UR123 / KH0363; Museum of Worldculture catalog 16.9.56-58.

v3_contribution.claim: First deep read of UR115 (alias UR126). PROPOSED CLASSIFICATION: event_register -- a boundary-demarcation event record. The single STRING cord (taqa) structurally isolated by 6 EMPTYs encodes the ritual boundary-utterance as minimal text surrounded by silence.

evidence.test: single STRING cord bracketed by 4+2 EMPTY cords -- ritual-utterance signature

v3_contribution.claim: THREE-PIECE ASSEMBLAGE HYPOTHESIS: UR115 is the BOUNDARY counterpart in a coherent Pachacamac land-grant triplet: UR123 (NAMES, 65 cords, lineage chima / chiki hanan-hurin) + UR115 (BOUNDARIES, 55 cords, taqa single word) + UR124 (QUANTITIES, 44 cords, unread). If UR124 confirms the quantities pattern, this would be the FIRST VALIDATED KHIPU LAND-GRANT ASSEMBLAGE in the corpus.

65 percent EMPTY ratio (19/29 L1 cords) is the HIGHEST EMPTY ratio in the current deep-read session. Places UR115 in the EVENT-REGISTER / RITUAL-INVOCATION signature cluster (alongside UR178-181 Cajamarquilla tiny rituals).

evidence.test: EMPTY cord count 19 of 29 L1 cords -- 65 percent segmentation

v3_contribution.claim: GG identity color appears on 10 cords in UR115 -- the SAME color family as UR123's 18 GG cords. The shared use of GG across the triplet's TWO KHIPUS suggests a scribal convention: GG marks the LINEAGE identity thread running through both the name register (UR123) and the boundary register (UR115).
v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (single STRING onset is L5 ta-, not L10 or L11). Confirms v3.1 non-perturbation on an event / boundary register.

companions.note: Sister khipu in the Goteborg three-piece set (16.9.56, 65 cords). UR123 is the NAMES REGISTER with 7 chi-compound lineages in hanan-hurin (4 chima + 3 chiki). UR115 is the BOUNDARY REGISTER (taqa x1). Read together, they form the NAMES + BOUNDARIES axes of a single land-grant.

Third sister in the Goteborg three-piece set (16.9.58, 44 cords). Unread in validated corpus as of 2026-04-17. PROPOSED: UR124 is the QUANTITIES REGISTER -- tribute / harvest / population counts for the lineages named in UR123 within the boundaries defined in UR115. Deep-read needed to confirm.
Cajamarquilla ritual_invocation (8 cords, high EMPTY). UR178 and UR115 share the high-EMPTY event-register signature -- tiny rituals encoded via silence.
Pachacamac ceque_registry. UR203's ceque-territorial documents name the huacas within boundaries; UR115 records a single boundary-marking event of the same sacred-geography type.
Pachacamac cadastral_survey (siqa x5 = pyramid ramps). UR1095 is the full Pachacamac cadastral document; UR115 is the minimal BOUNDARY-EVENT specialization of the same territorial tradition.
Validated event_register (Incahuasi). Closest analog in the event-register genre for comparing UR115's sparse structure.

word_reading.note: 1/1 STRING cord with 100 percent dict hit (taqa, standard Quechua).

document_type.note: event_register classification rests on the auto-classifier output + the 65 percent EMPTY ratio + the structurally isolated single STRING word. The BOUNDARY-EVENT sub-interpretation and the TRIPLET ASSEMBLAGE hypothesis are new and require UR124 replication. A possible alternative classification is cadastral_survey (given taqa is a cadastral word), but the extreme sparsity and ritual-silence structure favor event_register.

narrative.note: The boundary-ceremony narrative is internally coherent and externally anchored by Cobo 1653 and colonial visita boundary-dispute documents. The triplet assemblage hypothesis is plausible and specific enough to be testable via UR124 deep read -- but until that is done, the interpretation of UR115 as the BOUNDARY of UR123's lineage territory is provisional.

data_integrity.note: KFG twin KH0351, all cord values clean (1 declared sum unverified, but this is consistent with non-audit event-register genre). No L?, no DIVERGENT, no damage notes in OKR.

sections.title: Territorial Measurements
sections.reading: 18-cord main data block with alternating INT / EMPTY pattern. Nine numerical values (5, 60, 11, 1, 10, 10, 7, 7, 12 = 123 total) encode territorial measurements: perimeter stakes, subdivision counts, landmark distances. L2 subsidiaries carry secondary channel data on GG, W, and KB.

key_cords.gloss: AB INT 60 + L2 W 30 + L2 W 3
key_cords.note: largest single measurement -- possibly total territorial extent in standard units

sections.title: Separator
sections.reading: Single EMPTY cord marking the transition from the territorial data block to the boundary event.

key_cords.note: pivot separator between data and ceremonial word

sections.title: The Boundary Event (taqa)
sections.reading: Eight-cord cluster containing the document's ONE ceremonial word. Four opening EMPTYs (cords 20-22, 24) establish silence. Cord 23 carries an isolated INT (11) -- possibly the measurement of the boundary itself. Cord 25 carries the STRING taqa on W (white, territorial color). Two closing EMPTYs (cords 26-27) hold the silence after the utterance.

key_cords.note: THE BOUNDARY WORD -- bracketed by 4+2 EMPTY silences

sections.title: Closing Silence
sections.reading: Two final EMPTY cords on W. The document ends in silence after the boundary utterance.
